message:
feat: persistent polecats — stop nuking sandboxes after work completion (gt-4ac)

Polecats now persist after completing work instead of being destroyed.
The sandbox (worktree) is preserved and the polecat enters an idle state,
ready for reuse by the next gt sling. This eliminates worktree creation
overhead and preserves polecat identity across assignments.

Key changes:
- gt done: set agent state to "idle" instead of self-nuking worktree
- gt sling: reuse idle polecats before allocating new ones
- Witness: stop auto-nuking clean polecats (persistent model)
- Agent beads: non-ephemeral (issues table) for durable idle detection
- New StateIdle lifecycle state for polecats

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
